Keywords: saas, b2b, cloud, subscription, platform, software, general

Primary Personas: Solo User, Power User
Metric Focus: MRR, activation, weekly active users
Key Considerations: Speed and simplicity first. One job done extremely well. Avoid enterprise bloat.

Metric Focus: ARR, NPS, renewal rate, enterprise adoption, support ticket volume
Key Considerations: Security and compliance are table stakes. SAML/SSO required. Audit logs required. Change management is a feature.

Keywords: api, developer, platform, sdk, integration, cli, webhook, oauth
Sections Order: Problem > Developer Personas > Use Cases > API Spec > Error Handling > Rate Limits > Authentication > SDK > Docs > Launch
Primary Personas: Developer, Tech Lead, DevOps, Integration Engineer
Metric Focus: API adoption, time-to-first-call, error rate, SDK downloads, developer NPS
Key Considerations: Docs are the product. Error messages must be actionable. SDKs reduce friction. Versioning and deprecation matter.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Admin UI for API users, non-developer persona requirements

Keywords: plg, product-led, freemium, self-serve, viral, free trial, signup, onboarding
Sections Order: Problem > User Journey > Activation Moment > User Stories > Virality Hooks > Upgrade Triggers > Metrics > Out of Scope
Primary Personas: Free User, Converted User, Power User, Invitee
Metric Focus: Time-to-value, activation rate, free-to-paid conversion, viral coefficient, feature adoption
Key Considerations: The product IS the sales funnel. Identify the activation moment early. Friction at signup kills everything. Build upgrade nudges contextually.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Enterprise-only features, manual sales process, high-touch onboarding

Keywords: ai, ml, machine learning, artificial intelligence, llm, gpt, model, prediction, automation, recommendation
Sections Order: Problem > AI Use Case > User Intent > Model Behavior > Acceptance Criteria > Edge Cases > Fallback > Guardrails > Metrics > Out of Scope
Primary Personas: AI Consumer User, Admin, Model Reviewer
Metric Focus: Task completion rate, accuracy/quality score, latency, user override rate, trust score
Key Considerations: Define desired outcomes not model internals. Always specify fallback when confidence is low. Users need to understand AI decisions. Privacy and data usage must be explicit.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Model training details, specific ML architecture, internal tooling for model ops

Keywords: marketplace, two-sided, buyer, seller, vendor, network, listing, transaction
Sections Order: Problem > Two-Sided Personas > Supply vs Demand Balance > Transaction Flow > Trust & Safety > Metrics > Out of Scope
Primary Personas: Buyer, Seller/Vendor, Marketplace Admin
Metric Focus: GMV, take rate, supply-demand ratio, listing quality, trust score, repeat transaction rate
Key Considerations: Solve both sides simultaneously. Trust and safety are table stakes. Network effects define value. Cold start problem must be addressed.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Payment processing internals, tax compliance details, international expansion specifics

Keywords: healthcare, medical, clinic, patient, ehr, hipaa, telemedicine, pharma
Sections Order: Problem > Regulatory Context > Personas > Workflows > Compliance > Requirements > Privacy > Design > Technical > Launch
Primary Personas: Clinician, Patient, Admin, Billing Staff, Compliance Officer
Metric Focus: Patient outcomes, clinical workflow efficiency, HIPAA compliance rate, time savings per encounter
Key Considerations: HIPAA compliance is non-negotiable. Workflow must mirror clinical reality. Avoid disrupting existing EHR workflows unless explicitly replacing them.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Medical device certification, FDA clearance requirements, payer integration

Keywords: fintech, financial, banking, payment, lending, compliance, kyc, aml, pci
Sections Order: Problem > Regulatory Context > Personas > Compliance Requirements > User Stories > Security > Technical > Audit > Launch
Primary Personas: Consumer, Business Owner, Compliance Officer, Finance Manager
Metric Focus: Transaction success rate, fraud rate, time-to-approval, compliance pass rate, AUM/TPV
Key Considerations: Regulatory compliance shapes every decision. Security and fraud prevention are mandatory. Audit trails required. Performance under high transaction load is critical.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Core banking integration details, specific regulatory filings, multi-jurisdiction rollout

Keywords: edtech, education, learning, lms, course, student, instructor, school
Sections Order: Problem > Learner Personas > Learning Goals > Engagement Design > Requirements > Metrics > Content Strategy > Launch
Primary Personas: Student/Learner, Instructor, L&D Admin, Institution Admin
Metric Focus: Course completion rate, assessment scores, engagement rate, time-in-product, certification achieved
Key Considerations: Engagement and completion are the core metrics. Accessibility (WCAG) is critical. Content formats must support diverse learning styles. Progress tracking motivates behavior.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Curriculum authoring details, content licensing, third-party integrations outside scope

Keywords: hr, human resources, people, payroll, hiring, ats, onboarding, performance, engagement
Sections Order: Problem > HR Personas > Employee Journey > Workflow > Compliance > Requirements > Design > Launch
Primary Personas: HR Admin, Manager, Employee, Recruiter, Payroll Specialist
Metric Focus: Time-to-hire, employee NPS (eNPS), payroll accuracy rate, onboarding completion rate
Key Considerations: Employee data is sensitive — privacy and role-based access are mandatory. Workflows must align to HR calendar (payroll cycles, review periods). Compliance by region matters.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Payroll tax calculation internals, benefits carrier integrations, jurisdiction-specific rules

Keywords: data, analytics, bi, business intelligence, reporting, dashboard, warehouse, pipeline
Sections Order: Problem > Data Consumer Personas > Key Questions to Answer > Data Model > Visualization > Performance > Access Control > Out of Scope
Primary Personas: Analyst, Data Engineer, Business Stakeholder, Executive
Metric Focus: Dashboard adoption rate, query performance (p95), data freshness, self-serve rate, time-to-insight
Key Considerations: Answer specific business questions, not just display data. Performance at scale is a feature. Self-serve lowers data team burden. Guardrail: data accuracy must not regress.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: ETL pipeline engineering, data warehouse architecture, real-time streaming (unless specified)

Keywords: internal, admin, ops, back-office, tooling, dashboard, workflow
Sections Order: Problem > Internal User Personas > Key Workflows > Requirements > Access Control > Out of Scope
Primary Personas: Operations Staff, Customer Support, Finance, Engineering
Metric Focus: Task completion time, error rate, manual work eliminated, support resolution time
Key Considerations: Speed over aesthetics. Solve the exact workflow problem. Audit trail and access control are usually required. Avoid over-engineering.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Customer-facing changes, API endpoints for external use, high availability SLA

Keywords: mobile, app, ios, android, smartphone, phone, tablet, native, cross-platform, react native, flutter, swift, kotlin
Sections Order: Problem > Mobile User Context > Personas > App Journey Map > Activation Moment > User Stories > Push & Engagement > Offline Strategy > Platform Requirements (iOS/Android) > Metrics > Edge Cases > App Store Compliance > Launch & Rollout
Primary Personas: Mobile End User, Power User, Admin (web), Free Tier User
Metric Focus: DAU/MAU ratio, D1/D7/D30 retention, session length, crash-free rate, app store rating, push opt-in rate, app size
Key Considerations: Mobile-first means mobile-only thinking. Every feature must work on a 5-inch screen. Offline is not optional. Push notifications are your re-engagement lifeline. App store review guidelines constrain everything. Performance budget: cold start <2s, app size <50MB.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Desktop feature parity, admin panel in mobile app, complex data entry forms, real-time streaming (use polling), tablet-specific layouts in V1

Keywords: mobile marketplace, on-demand, delivery, rideshare, food delivery, service marketplace, gig, booking, reservation
Sections Order: Problem > Two-Sided Mobile Personas > Location/Map Flow > Transaction Flow > Trust & Safety > Push & Notification Strategy > Offline Resilience > Metrics > Edge Cases > Launch
Primary Personas: Buyer/Consumer, Seller/Provider, Admin
Metric Focus: GMV, take rate, order completion rate, time-to-match, provider utilization, D7 retention, app store rating
Key Considerations: Location and real-time are central. Both sides need a mobile experience. Trust signals (ratings, reviews, verification) are critical on mobile. Push notifications drive engagement on both sides. Payment must be frictionless (Apple Pay, Google Pay).
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Web dashboard for providers, complex reporting, multi-country expansion, payment processor integration details

Keywords: mobile fintech, mobile banking, mobile payment, digital wallet, neobank, investment app, crypto, trading, transfer, money
Sections Order: Problem > Regulatory Context > Mobile Personas > Security Architecture > KYC/AML Flow > Transaction Flow > Biometric Auth > Push Alerts > Compliance > Offline Behavior > Metrics > Launch
Primary Personas: Consumer, Business User, Compliance Officer
Metric Focus: Transaction success rate, fraud rate, MAU, session frequency, biometric auth adoption, app store rating, time-to-first-transaction
Key Considerations: Security is THE product. Biometric auth is mandatory. Every screen must consider screenshot blocking for sensitive data. PCI-DSS and local regulations constrain design. Transaction confirmation flows must prevent accidental sends. Push for transaction alerts is expected.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Core banking integration details, regulatory filings, multi-jurisdiction rollout, crypto custody architecture

Keywords: mobile health, wellness, fitness, meditation, sleep, mental health, health tracker, telehealth, patient app, wearable
Sections Order: Problem > Health Context > Patient/User Personas > Health Data Flow > Privacy & Compliance (HIPAA if applicable) > Wearable Integration > Notification Strategy > Offline Data Capture > Metrics > Edge Cases > Launch
Primary Personas: Patient/User, Healthcare Provider, Caregiver, Admin
Metric Focus: Daily active users, health metric tracking adherence, session completion rate, D30 retention, app store rating, NPS, wearable sync success rate
Key Considerations: Health data is maximally sensitive — encryption everywhere, minimal data collection. Wearable integration (HealthKit, Google Fit) is often expected. Offline data capture and sync is critical for field use. Accessibility is non-negotiable (elderly users, visual impairments). Notifications must be helpful not anxiety-inducing.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Medical device certification, FDA compliance (unless required), EHR integration, clinical trial data management

Keywords: social, community, chat, messaging, feed, content, creator, ugc, video, photo, sharing, social network, forum
Sections Order: Problem > Social Context > Creator & Consumer Personas > Content Flow > Feed Algorithm > Moderation & Safety > Push & Engagement Loop > Viral Mechanics > Metrics > Edge Cases > Launch
Primary Personas: Content Creator, Consumer/Viewer, Moderator, Admin
Metric Focus: DAU/MAU, time-in-app, content creation rate, viral coefficient, D1/D7 retention, report-to-action time, app store rating
Key Considerations: Content moderation is not optional — plan for it from day 1. Feed ranking defines the product experience. Push notifications drive the engagement loop. Camera/media quality matters. Viral mechanics (sharing, invites) are growth levers. Real-time features (chat, live) need careful performance planning.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Web app feature parity, admin moderation dashboard (separate project), advanced recommendation algorithm, monetization/ads framework

Keywords: ecommerce, shopping, retail, store, cart, checkout, product catalog, order, mobile commerce, m-commerce
Sections Order: Problem > Shopping Context > Buyer Personas > Product Discovery Flow > Cart & Checkout > Payment Integration > Push & Re-engagement > Order Tracking > Metrics > Edge Cases > Launch
Primary Personas: Shopper, Returning Customer, Guest Buyer, Merchant/Admin
Metric Focus: Conversion rate, cart abandonment rate, AOV, D30 retention, push-driven revenue, app store rating, session-to-purchase time
Key Considerations: Checkout must be <3 taps from cart. Apple Pay / Google Pay are table stakes. Push for abandoned carts and price drops drives revenue. Product images must load fast on slow connections. Search and discovery UX is critical on small screens. Guest checkout is required.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Inventory management system, warehouse logistics, supplier portal, full CMS for product pages

Keywords: mobile learning, mobile education, language app, course app, quiz, flashcard, study, tutoring, mobile lms
Sections Order: Problem > Learning Context > Learner Personas > Learning Path Design > Session Flow > Gamification & Streaks > Push & Habit Formation > Offline Content > Metrics > Edge Cases > Launch
Primary Personas: Student/Learner, Instructor, Parent (if K-12), L&D Admin
Metric Focus: Lesson completion rate, streak length, D7/D30 retention, daily session count, learning outcome improvement, app store rating
Key Considerations: Habit formation is the product strategy. Streaks and gamification drive retention. Content must work offline (downloaded lessons). Sessions should be short (5-15 min) for mobile. Push notifications for streak reminders are critical. Progress visualization motivates continued use.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Full LMS admin panel, content authoring tool, assessment certification system, institution management

Keywords: productivity, utility, tool, task, calendar, note, document, scanner, calculator, timer, organizer, planner
Sections Order: Problem > Utility Context > User Personas > Core Workflow > Widget & Quick Actions > Offline First > Metrics > Out of Scope > Launch
Primary Personas: Daily User, Power User, Free Tier User
Metric Focus: DAU/MAU, session frequency, task completion rate, widget usage, D30 retention, app store rating, conversion to premium
Key Considerations: One job done extremely well. Cold start must be <1s. Widget support (iOS/Android) extends utility beyond the app. Offline-first is mandatory for productivity tools. Minimal UI — every tap counts. Keyboard shortcuts and accessibility are differentiators.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Desktop app, web app, team collaboration features, third-party integrations beyond core

Keywords: super app, multi-service, all-in-one, mini program, platform, ride food pay, multi-feature
Sections Order: Problem > Platform Context > Service Map > User Personas per Service > Cross-Service Flow > Wallet/Payment > Push Strategy per Service > Performance Budget > Metrics per Service > Launch Strategy
Primary Personas: Consumer, Service Provider, Merchant, Platform Admin
Metric Focus: MAU across services, cross-service adoption rate, wallet adoption, transaction volume, D30 retention, app size, cold start time
Key Considerations: App size is the #1 risk — use modular loading. Each mini-service must feel native. Cross-service discovery drives platform value. Single wallet/payment reduces friction. Push strategy must not spam — coordinate across services. Performance budget per module is critical.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Individual service admin panels, merchant onboarding portal, regulatory compliance per service vertical, payment processor integration details

Keywords: companion app, mobile extension, web-first mobile, mobile add-on, sync, mobile view, mobile access
Sections Order: Problem > Mobile Context (when/where) > Personas > Mobile-Specific Use Cases > Sync Strategy > Push Notifications > Offline Subset > Metrics > Out of Scope > Launch
Primary Personas: Mobile User (existing web user), Admin (web only), Field Worker
Metric Focus: Mobile DAU / Web DAU ratio, mobile-specific feature adoption, sync success rate, push-driven return-to-web, app store rating
Key Considerations: Mobile companion is NOT a responsive web app — it's a purpose-built tool for mobile contexts (commute, field, meeting). Identify the 3-5 things users need on mobile and do only those. Sync must be seamless. Push notifications bridge mobile ↔ web engagement.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Full feature parity with web app, admin features, complex data entry, reporting/analytics dashboards

Keywords: ai mobile, ai app, ai assistant, chatbot app, ai camera, ai photo, ai voice, ai recommendation, on-device ai, mobile ml
Sections Order: Problem > AI Use Case on Mobile > User Personas > Model Behavior Spec > On-Device vs Cloud Decision > Confidence & Fallback > Privacy & Data > Push & Re-engagement > Offline AI Capability > Metrics > Edge Cases > Launch
Primary Personas: AI Consumer User, Power User, Privacy-Conscious User
Metric Focus: Task completion rate, AI accuracy/quality score, latency (on-device vs cloud), user override rate, D7 retention, app store rating, privacy opt-in rate
Key Considerations: On-device vs cloud is the fundamental architecture decision. Latency expectation is <500ms for on-device, <2s for cloud. Privacy is paramount — be explicit about what data leaves the device. Fallback when AI confidence is low must be graceful. Model size impacts app size and download time.
Common Out-of-Scope Mistakes: Model training pipeline, ML ops infrastructure, A/B testing framework for model variants, data labeling tools
